From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Former trade union of the United Kingdom

Municipal Employees' Association

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Merged into | [National Union of General and Municipal Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_General_and_Municipal_Workers "National Union of General and Municipal Workers") |
| Founded | 1888 |
| Dissolved | 1924 |
| Headquarters | 24 Fitzjohns Road, [Hampstead](/wiki/Hampstead "Hampstead"), London |
| Location | * United Kingdom |
| Members | 65,000 (1921) |
| Key people | [Albin Taylor](/wiki/Albin_Taylor "Albin Taylor") [Peter J. Tevenan](/wiki/Peter_J._Tevenan "Peter J. Tevenan") |
| Affiliations | [TUC](/wiki/Trades_Union_Congress "Trades Union Congress"), [ITUC](/wiki/Irish_Trades_Union_Congress "Irish Trades Union Congress"), [Labour](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") |

The **Municipal Employees' Association** was a [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") representing local government workers in the United Kingdom.

The union was founded in 1888 as the **London County Council Employees' Protection Association**, to represent workers at the [London County Council](/wiki/London_County_Council "London County Council"), which was formally constituted the following year.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The union was initially led by William Anderson.[[2]](#cite_note-marsh-2)

In 1899 it was renamed the **National Association of County Authority Employees**. It grew considerably after the collapse of the [National Municipal Labour Union](/w/index.php?title=National_Municipal_Labour_Union&action=edit&redlink=1 "National Municipal Labour Union (page does not exist)") in 1900, and in 1901 it became the "Municipal Employees Association". In 1907, General Secretary [Albin Taylor](/wiki/Albin_Taylor "Albin Taylor") was dismissed by the union's General Council. The following year, he set up a break-away [National Union of Corporation Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Corporation_Workers "National Union of Corporation Workers"). Nevertheless, by 1910, the Association had 13,500 members.[[2]](#cite_note-marsh-2) Membership continued to grow under new general secretary [Peter J. Tevenan](/wiki/Peter_J._Tevenan "Peter J. Tevenan"), reaching a peak of 65,000 in 1921, with about one-fifth in London, and a further fifth in Scotland and Ireland.[[2]](#cite_note-marsh-2)

In 1924, the Association merged with the [National Union of General Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_General_Workers_(UK) "National Union of General Workers (UK)") and the [National Amalgamated Union of Labour](/wiki/National_Amalgamated_Union_of_Labour "National Amalgamated Union of Labour") to form the [National Union of General and Municipal Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_General_and_Municipal_Workers "National Union of General and Municipal Workers").[[2]](#cite_note-marsh-2)

## General Secretaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Municipal_Employees%27_Association&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: General Secretaries")]

:   1894: William Anderson
:   1902: [Albin Taylor](/wiki/Albin_Taylor "Albin Taylor")
:   1907: [Richard Davies](/wiki/Richard_Davies_(trade_unionist) "Richard Davies (trade unionist)")
:   1913: [Peter J. Tevenan](/wiki/Peter_J._Tevenan "Peter J. Tevenan")
